executiveSummary: Instagram OAuth implementation has CRITICAL security vulnerabilities that MUST be fixed before production deployment. Primary concerns: (1) UPSERT race condition allows duplicate accounts, (2) Health worker lacks workspace isolation, (3) Refresh worker lacks distributed locking, (4) Token logging in multiple locations, (5) Missing cross-workspace leakage protection.

criticalIssues.title: UPSERT Implementation Vulnerable to Race Condition
criticalIssues.description: Two concurrent OAuth callbacks can create duplicate accounts due to non-atomic UPSERT

vulnerability.code:
const existingAccount = await SocialAccount.findOne(...);
if (existingAccount) { update } else { create }
vulnerability.issue: Time gap between findOne() and create() allows race condition
vulnerability.scenario: User clicks 'Connect Instagram' twice rapidly → Two callbacks execute simultaneously → Both findOne() return null → Both create() succeed → Duplicate accounts with same (workspaceId, provider, providerUserId)
vulnerability.uniqueIndexBehavior: Unique index will throw E11000 error on second insert, but error is not handled gracefully

criticalIssues.impact: HIGH - Duplicate accounts corrupt database, break billing, confuse users
criticalIssues.exploitability: EASY - User can trigger by double-clicking or browser back button
criticalIssues.fix: Use MongoDB findOneAndUpdate with upsert:true option for atomic UPSERT

criticalIssues.title: Health Check Worker Not Workspace-Scoped
criticalIssues.description: AccountHealthCheckWorker queries ALL active accounts across ALL workspaces without isolation

vulnerability.code: const accounts = await SocialAccount.find({ status: AccountStatus.ACTIVE }).select('+accessToken');
vulnerability.issue: Missing workspaceId filter - queries all workspaces globally
vulnerability.impact: Worker processes accounts from ALL tenants in single batch, no isolation

securityImplications: One tenant's API quota exhaustion affects all tenants
Potential cross-tenant data leakage in logs
criticalIssues.fix: Worker should process accounts per workspace with isolation

criticalIssues.title: Access Tokens Logged in Multiple Locations
criticalIssues.description: Tokens appear in console.log statements and structured logs

locations.issue: State parameter logged (not a token, but sensitive)
CRITICAL - Full state value logged to console
CRITICAL - Full token response logged (contains access_token)

criticalIssues.impact: CRITICAL - Tokens in logs can be extracted by attackers with log access
criticalIssues.fix: Remove ALL console.log statements, sanitize structured logs to exclude tokens

criticalIssues.title: Token Refresh Worker Lacks Distributed Lock
criticalIssues.description: Multiple worker instances can refresh same token concurrently, causing corruption

vulnerability.scenario: Two worker instances run simultaneously → Both find same expiring account → Both call refreshAccessToken() → Both save new token → Race condition on token version
vulnerability.issue: No distributed lock (Redis SETNX) to ensure only one worker refreshes per account
vulnerability.consequence: Token version mismatch, potential token corruption, failed refreshes

criticalIssues.fix: Implement Redis-based distributed lock per account before refresh

criticalIssues.title: revokeAccess() Missing Workspace Validation
criticalIssues.description: User can revoke accounts from other workspaces by guessing accountId

vulnerability.issue: No workspaceId validation - only checks accountId exists
vulnerability.exploit: Attacker in workspace A can revoke account from workspace B by guessing accountId

criticalIssues.fix: Add workspace validation: findOne({ _id: accountId, workspaceId })

highSeverityIssues.title: Account Type Validation After Token Exchange
highSeverityIssues.description: PERSONAL accounts rejected AFTER long-lived token exchange, wasting API quota
highSeverityIssues.issue: Validation happens in getUserProfile(), after both token exchanges complete
highSeverityIssues.impact: PERSONAL account users waste API quota, see confusing error after OAuth flow
highSeverityIssues.recommendation: Validate account_type in short-lived token response (if available) before long-lived exchange

highSeverityIssues.title: consumeStateForWorkspace() Not Used in Instagram Flow
highSeverityIssues.description: Instagram callback uses consumeState() without workspace validation

vulnerability.code: const stateData = await oauthStateService.consumeState(state as string);
vulnerability.issue: No workspace validation - state from workspace A could be used in workspace B context

highSeverityIssues.impact: MEDIUM - State already contains workspaceId, but no validation against request context
highSeverityIssues.recommendation: Add workspace validation in callback handler

highSeverityIssues.title: No Rollback on Partial Save Failure
highSeverityIssues.description: If tokenSafetyService.storeTokenMetadata() fails, account saved but metadata missing

vulnerability.scenario: account.save() succeeds → storeTokenMetadata() fails → Account exists but token metadata missing → Token refresh fails

highSeverityIssues.recommendation: Wrap in try-catch, delete account if metadata storage fails

mediumSeverityIssues.title: Refresh Worker Not Idempotent
mediumSeverityIssues.description: Worker can process same account multiple times if run overlaps
mediumSeverityIssues.issue: No tracking of 'currently refreshing' accounts, 12-hour interval may overlap
mediumSeverityIssues.recommendation: Add 'lastRefreshAttemptAt' field, skip if refreshed within last hour

mediumSeverityIssues.title: Health Worker Retries on 4xx Errors
mediumSeverityIssues.description: Worker should not retry on 401/403 (permanent failures)
mediumSeverityIssues.issue: No distinction between retryable (5xx) and non-retryable (4xx) errors
mediumSeverityIssues.recommendation: Only retry on 5xx, immediately mark REAUTH_REQUIRED on 4xx

violations: console.log('State full value:', state) - Line 78 OAuthStateService.ts
Multiple console.log statements with sensitive data

stateCannotBeReused.evidence: consumeState() uses atomic GETDEL, state deleted after first use

missingStateFails.evidence: consumeState() returns null if state not found, callback redirects with error

personalAccountsRejected.evidence: getUserProfile() validates account_type, throws error if not BUSINESS/CREATOR

tokensEncrypted.evidence: Pre-save hook encrypts tokens using AES-256-GCM before persistence

noMemoryFallback.evidence: Memory fallback removed, throws error if Redis unavailable

violations: Health worker queries all workspaces globally
No workspace validation in callback handler

workersScoped.evidence: InstagramTokenRefreshWorker and AccountHealthCheckWorker query globally without workspace isolation

noGlobalFetch.evidence: revokeAccess() uses findById(accountId) without workspaceId check

uniqueIndexEnforced.evidence: Unique index on (workspaceId, provider, providerUserId) exists at line 139-142 SocialAccount.ts

existingRowReused.evidence: connectAccount() finds existing account and updates it (lines 99-127)

noDuplicateInsert.evidence: Race condition allows duplicate inserts, unique index throws error but not handled

capabilitiesRecalculated.evidence: metadata.capabilities set to { publish: true, analytics: true, messaging: true } (lines 108-112)

errorCountersCleared.evidence: No error counter fields in schema

refreshOnlyWithinThreshold.evidence: Queries tokenExpiresAt < thresholdDate (7 days) at line 90

distributedLockPerAccount.evidence: No Redis SETNX lock before refresh operation

softDeleteOnly.evidence: Sets status to DISCONNECTED, no hard delete

encryptedTokenWiped.evidence: Token not wiped, only status changed. Encrypted token remains in database.

disconnectedAtSet.evidence: No disconnectedAt field in schema or disconnect logic

retryLogicCorrect.evidence: No retry logic implemented, should not retry on 4xx

noAggressiveRetry.evidence: No retry logic at all (which is correct for 4xx)

duplicateAccountPrevention.evidence: Two concurrent callbacks CAN create duplicates due to non-atomic UPSERT

tokenCorruptionPrevention.evidence: Two refresh workers CAN corrupt token due to missing distributed lock

stateReplayImpossible.evidence: Atomic GETDEL ensures state consumed only once

missingEnterpriseGradeSafeguards.description: Redis SETNX lock per account to prevent concurrent refresh
missingEnterpriseGradeSafeguards.implementation: Use Redis SET key value NX EX ttl before refresh operation

missingEnterpriseGradeSafeguards.description: Workers should process accounts per workspace with isolation
missingEnterpriseGradeSafeguards.implementation: Add workspaceId to worker queries, implement per-workspace scheduling
Replace findOne + create/update with single atomic operation

missingEnterpriseGradeSafeguards.description: Overwrite encrypted token with random data before soft delete
Every account operation must validate workspaceId
missingEnterpriseGradeSafeguards.implementation: Add workspaceId parameter to all service methods, validate in queries

missingEnterpriseGradeSafeguards.description: OAuth operations should be rate-limited per workspace, not globally
missingEnterpriseGradeSafeguards.implementation: Use Redis rate limiter with workspace-scoped keys

missingEnterpriseGradeSafeguards.description: Prevent duplicate account creation from browser retries
missingEnterpriseGradeSafeguards.implementation: Generate idempotency key, store in Redis, check before processing

missingEnterpriseGradeSafeguards.description: Prevent cascading failures when Instagram API is down
missingEnterpriseGradeSafeguards.implementation: Track failure rate, open circuit after threshold, retry with backoff

hardeningImprovements.rationale: Console logs may contain sensitive data, use structured logger only
Prevent race condition in concurrent OAuth callbacks

hardeningImprovements.improvement: Add distributed lock to token refresh
hardeningImprovements.rationale: Prevent token corruption from concurrent refresh operations
Ensure multi-tenant isolation in background jobs
Reduce attack surface for disconnected accounts
Track when account was disconnected for audit purposes
Prevent one tenant from exhausting OAuth quota

blockers: CRITICAL-001: UPSERT race condition allows duplicate accounts
CRITICAL-003: Tokens logged in console.log statements
CRITICAL-004: Token refresh lacks distributed locking
mustFixBeforeProduction: Add distributed locking to token refresh
Add workspace validation to all operations
